summary: Executed backend-only static regression tests for onboarding stabilisation and Xero callback scope. All 5 scoped static checks passed; one critical runtime redirect risk identified in requireOrg.

critical.issue: redirect('/login') is called inside try/catch. In Next.js, redirect() throws; catch swallows it and always redirects to '/onboarding', causing unauthenticated users to be routed incorrectly.

minor.issue: Could not execute live endpoint verification in this environment: no reachable public URL variable found and localhost:3000 was unreachable (connection failed).

action_items: Fix requireOrg to preserve redirect('/login') semantics (rethrow NEXT_REDIRECT or avoid catch around redirect paths).
After fix, run live auth/org routing smoke tests with a reachable app URL.
Optionally add runtime integration tests for /api/xero/callback and onboarding/import-review server flows.
critical_code_review_comments: Critical control-flow bug: catching redirect() throwable in requireOrg changes intended login redirect behavior.
Onboarding action currently tolerates some org update failures and may continue with partial org field updates; validate whether this silent degradation is acceptable.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Scoped static verification is complete and green. Priority retest should focus on requireOrg runtime behavior with unauthenticated session and missing org states, then validate xero callback redirect behavior on a reachable deployed URL.
rca of the issue: RCA: In Next.js App Router, redirect() throws a framework-controlled error to stop execution. Wrapping redirect calls in broad try/catch (requireOrg) captures that throwable. Current catch then forces redirect('/onboarding'), overriding intended redirect('/login') branch. Repro (runtime): call requireOrg with unauthenticated session -> redirect('/login') thrown -> caught -> redirected to /onboarding. Mitigation: either remove try/catch around redirect paths, or detect and rethrow redirect errors before fallback handling.
